what_it_does: Defines the EXTRACT_URL seam for the recollection distill map step: a flat ExtractRecord contract, a deterministic in-process stub used by default, and a fail-loud HTTP extractor path that enforces the same record shape via a strict JSON Schema response_format.
observations: EXTRACT_URL env variable toggles between the in-process stub and a served model endpoint; unset means no model load.
ExtractRecord is a total flat shape: every field is a string, optional signals default to empty string, and pattern_tag is the only always-required cluster key.
The served path uses response_format type json_schema with strict:true, not json_object.
servedExtract throws ExtractError on network failure, non-OK HTTP, or non-JSON responses, with no fallback to the stub.
coerceRecord accepts either a bare record object or an OpenAI-shaped choices[0].message.content JSON string.
The stub derives decision from the assistant response and pattern_tag from leading words, leaving all judgment fields empty.
EXTRACT_MODEL is configurable via env, defaulting to cyankiwi/Qwen3.5-4B-AWQ-4bit.
extractOne accepts an optional urlOverride for tests, but production behavior is driven by env.
standouts: The explicit model-gate beat: default stub avoids loading a 4B model until explicitly greenlit.
Fail-loud design for the served extractor, refusing silent degradation to the stub.
Strict json_schema enforced-output contract intended to make stub-to-real swap byte-shaped-identical.
conventions: Env-driven URL seam pattern, consistent with embeddings and other configurable model boundaries.
Snake_case field names in TypeScript interfaces and a total shape with empty-string defaults.
Test-only override parameter kept separate from the production env seam.
Custom error class named after the operation (ExtractError) for localized diagnostics.
concerns: coerceRecord silently drops unknown extra keys after requesting additionalProperties:false, which could hide minor model noncompliance.
servedExtract has no request timeout, retry, or backoff policy.
pattern_tag is required by contract but coerceRecord only checks it is a string, so an empty served value would pass.
notes: This is the configurable extraction boundary for the recollection map step: a deterministic stub by default and a strict-schema, fail-loud served extractor when EXTRACT_URL is set.
questions: What is the exact request contract expected by the served endpoint beyond the OpenAI/vLLM-like shape used here?
How does downstream assembly map ExtractRecord fields to kebab unit_types via FIELD_TO_UNIT_TYPE?
What gate controls whether recollection-only fields (principle, concept_definition, what_built) are assembled?
Is there a planned retry/backoff policy for transient served extractor failures?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to be a configurable EXTRACT_URL seam with a deterministic stub and a fail-loud served extractor using enforced JSON schema; the implementation matches that purpose.
intention_signals: It tries to let the recollection pipeline run and be tested without loading a 4B model, while making the switch to a real served model a single config change that enforces the same ExtractRecord shape.
novelty: The explicit model-gate beat that caches the model load until greenlit, combined with a byte-identical stub-to-real swap via strict json_schema response_format, is a deliberate seam design.
contribution: A self-contained extraction boundary that defines a total ExtractRecord contract, a deterministic in-process stub for model-free operation, and a fail-loud HTTP path for a served extractor, so downstream assembly can depend on a stable shape regardless of which side of the seam is active.
summary_for_embedding: TypeScript module implementing the EXTRACT_URL seam for the recollection distill map step, providing the ExtractRecord schema, a deterministic model-free stub, and a strict-schema fail-loud served extractor.
intention_for_embedding: To keep the recollection extraction stage runnable without loading a large model by default, while making the real 4B extractor a configuration-only re-point with an enforced output contract.

symbols.does: Defines the total flat record shape for one exchange's extracted signals.
symbols.description: ExtractRecord is the per-exchange output of the map step: all string fields default to empty, pattern_tag is the required cluster key, and the three recollection-only fields extend the base MineExchangeOut shape.

symbols.does: Defines the input shape for a single exchange passed to the extractor.
symbols.description: ExchangeUnit carries tim_message, my_response, and optional session_id and ts, matching the run_items MAP shape so one extractor role can process many exchanges.

symbols.does: Custom error class for served extractor failures.
symbols.description: ExtractError is thrown when a configured EXTRACT_URL is unreachable, returns an HTTP error, or yields a malformed extract, making failures visible rather than silently falling back.

symbols.does: Returns a fresh ExtractRecord with every field set to empty string.
symbols.description: emptyRecord establishes the no-fiction default state where absence is represented by an empty string rather than omitted fields.

symbols.does: Reports whether the model-free stub is active.
symbols.description: isStubExtractor returns true when EXTRACT_URL is unset, indicating no served model is configured and the in-process stub will be used.

symbols.does: Reports the configured extractor endpoint for diagnostics.
symbols.description: extractorEndpoint returns EXTRACT_URL when set, otherwise the sentinel '(in-process stub)'.

symbols.does: Public seam that routes an exchange to stub or served extractor.
symbols.description: extractOne chooses stubExtract when no URL is configured and servedExtract when EXTRACT_URL or a test-only urlOverride is provided.

symbols.does: Deterministic model-free extractor that proves pipeline shape.
symbols.description: stubExtract records the assistant response as decision and derives a kebab pattern_tag, leaving all judgment-sensitive fields empty to avoid fabricating Tim-facing signals.

symbols.does: POSTs the exchange to a served extractor with enforced JSON schema output.
symbols.description: servedExtract sends the exchange in an input field with a strict json_schema response_format and throws ExtractError on transport, HTTP, or JSON parse failures.

symbols.does: Validates and coerces a served response into an ExtractRecord.
symbols.description: coerceRecord handles either a bare object or an OpenAI-shaped choices[0].message.content JSON string, requiring every ExtractRecord field to be a string and throwing otherwise.

symbols.does: Truncates a string to n characters with an ellipsis.
symbols.description: truncate is a deterministic helper used by the stub to cap the recorded decision length.

symbols.does: Builds a short kebab-case tag from leading words.
symbols.description: kebabTag is a deterministic helper that produces a stable pattern_tag for clustering across re-runs.
